summary: DHH highlights Mauricio Fernández's work on Ruby Production Archive (RPA), an alternative to RubyGems for packaging Ruby libraries. While DHH personally prefers Gems, he credits Mauricio for the significant effort of packaging both Instiki and Rails for RPA. He also notes that Mauricio created an installation video for Rails via RPA, inspired by the success of DHH's own Rails demo video.
key_points: RPA (Ruby Production Archive) existed as a competing packaging system to RubyGems in Ruby's early days
Packaging Rails was a non-trivial task even DHH hadn't tackled for GEMs yet
Community members were already creating demo videos and packaging for Rails shortly after its introduction
money_quotes: Especially the latter is quite a task (which is why I haven't done a GEM for it myself, yet).
He taped the whole thing through his prompt using Links to access the web-server. Very retro.
key_insight: Even in Rails' earliest days, community contributors were stepping up to solve packaging and evangelism challenges that the core team hadn't yet addressed.
